intro.en: The timeless capital where imperial grandeur meets cutting-edge modernity. Walk the Great Wall at sunrise, get lost in hutong alleyways, and taste Peking duck carved tableside.
intro.zh: 千年帝都，气象万千。长城日出、胡同深处、桌前现片的烤鸭——北京在古老与先锋之间自如呼吸。

desc.en: The most breathtaking section near Beijing — fewer crowds, restored yet authentic, with a thrilling toboggan ride down.
desc.zh: 北京近郊最壮美的一段——游客较少，修缮得当又不失古意，下山还能坐滑道。

tips.en: Go early — arrive by 8 AM to have the wall almost to yourself.
tips.zh: 早点去——8点前到，长城几乎是你的私人领地。

tags.zh: 世界遗产 · 地标 · 一日游

desc.en: The world's largest palace complex — 980 buildings, 600 years of history. Every courtyard whispers tales of emperors and dynasties.
desc.zh: 世界最大宫殿群——980座建筑，600年风云。每一进院落都是帝王将相的无声史书。

highlight.en: Hall of Supreme Harmony rooftop beasts

tips.en: Book online 7 days ahead — tickets sell out fast.
tips.zh: 提前7天网上预约——票抢得很快。

tags.zh: 世界遗产 · 必去 · 半日游

desc.en: Where emperors prayed for harvest — the Hall of Prayer for Good Harvests is China's most perfect wooden structure. Come early morning for locals practicing tai chi.
desc.zh: 帝王祭天之所——祈年殿是中国最完美的木构建筑。清晨来，看北京大爷大妈打太极。

tips.en: The Echo Wall really works — stand at one end and whisper.
tips.zh: 回音壁真的能回音——站一端轻声说话试试。

location.en: Dongcheng District, south of Forbidden City

tags.zh: 世界遗产 · 清晨 · 建筑

desc.en: Crispy skin, tender meat, carved tableside. Wrap in paper-thin pancakes with hoisin, cucumber, and scallions.
desc.zh: 皮脆肉嫩，桌边现片。薄饼一抹甜面酱，配上黄瓜条、葱丝，一卷入口。

where.en: Available at traditional roast duck restaurants citywide
where.zh: 各大传统烤鸭店均有供应

must_try.en: Ask for the 'crispy skin dipped in sugar' — a Beijing secret.
must_try.zh: 记得要'皮蘸白糖'——北京人的隐藏吃法。

desc.en: Hand-pulled noodles with fermented soybean paste, julienned cucumber, radish, and soybeans. Beijing's ultimate comfort food.
desc.zh: 手擀面配黄酱肉丁，黄瓜丝、萝卜丝、黄豆码得齐齐整整。北京人的灵魂碳水。

where.en: Found in neighborhood noodle shops — look for the red lanterns
where.zh: 胡同面馆随处可见——认准红灯笼招牌

must_try.en: Mix thoroughly before eating — every strand should be coated.
must_try.zh: 上桌赶紧拌——每根面都要裹满酱。

desc.en: The real Beijing lives in its alleyways. Nanluoguxiang for the buzz, Wudaoying for the calm. Rent a bicycle — it's the only way.
desc.zh: 胡同里才是真正的北京。南锣鼓巷热闹，五道营清静。租辆自行车——这是唯一的正确打开方式。

significance.en: Living history — some hutongs date back to the Yuan Dynasty (13th century)
significance.zh: 活着的历史——有些胡同可追溯至元朝（13世纪）

best_time.zh: 傍晚时分，金色光线最美

experience.zh: 租车骑行+胡同早点巡游

climate.en: Spring (Apr–May) & Autumn (Sep–Oct) — mild, blue skies, perfect for walking.
climate.zh: 春秋两季最佳（4-5月、9-10月）——温和舒适，蓝天白云，最适合步行探索。

transit.en: Subway covers all major sites (¥3–9/ride). Didi (Chinese Uber) is cheap and reliable. Avoid taxis during rush hour.
transit.zh: 地铁覆盖主要景区（¥3–9/次）。滴滴打车便宜可靠。高峰期避开出租车。

intercity.en: Beijing Capital (PEK) & Daxing (PKX) airports. High-speed rail: 4.5h to Shanghai, 2.5h to Xi'an.
intercity.zh: 首都机场(PEK)+大兴机场(PKX)。高铁：4.5小时到上海，2.5小时到西安。

tips.en: Carry your passport at all times. Download Alipay before arrival. VPN is essential — Google and Instagram are blocked. Tap water is NOT drinkable.
tips.zh: 随身带护照。提前下载支付宝。VPN必备——Google和Instagram被墙。自来水不可直饮。

practicalGuide.en:
Visa: Beijing has 144-hour transit visa-free for citizens of 54 countries. You must arrive via PEK or Daxing airport and depart to a third country (not your origin). Apply at the visa counter before immigration — have your next flight confirmation ready.

Payment: Download Alipay or WeChat Pay before arriving. Link your international credit card. Taxis, most restaurants, and even street vendors accept digital payment. Still carry ¥300–500 cash as backup — some small hutongs shops are cash-only.

SIM Card: Buy at the airport upon arrival. China Unicom and China Mobile both offer tourist SIMs (7/15/30 days, ¥100–300). You need your passport. Alternatively, get an eSIM before arriving (e.g., Holafly, Airalo).

VPN: Install a VPN on your phone AND laptop before arriving in China. Google, Instagram, Twitter, WhatsApp, and YouTube are all blocked. The VPN that works at home may NOT work in China — test it before you leave.

Language survival: Almost no one speaks English outside tourist areas. Download Google Translate (offline Chinese pack) or Pleco before arrival. Learn these: "谢谢" (xièxiè, thank you), "多少钱" (duōshao qián, how much), "不要辣" (bú yào là, no spice).
practicalGuide.zh:
签证：北京对54个国家公民实施144小时过境免签。必须从首都机场或大兴机场入境，前往第三国（不能回出发国）。在边检前到签证柜台办理——准备好下一程机票。

支付：出发前下载支付宝或微信支付，绑定国际信用卡。出租车、大部分餐厅、甚至街边摊都支持扫码支付。随身带¥300–500现金备用——有些胡同小店只收现金。

手机卡：下飞机后在机场买。中国联通和中国移动都有旅行卡（7/15/30天，¥100–300）。需要护照。或者出发前买eSIM（如Holafly、Airalo）。

VPN：在中国入境前就在手机和笔记本电脑上都装好VPN。Google、Instagram、Twitter、WhatsApp、YouTube全部被墙。你在家能用的VPN在中国可能不行——出发前一定要测试。

语言救命：景区以外几乎没人说英语。出发前下载Google翻译（离线中文包）或Pleco。记住这几句：谢谢、多少钱、不要辣。

honestTips.en:
The Great Wall is crowded. Everyone goes to Badaling — it's a human river. Go to Mutianyu instead (fewer crowds, toboggan ride). Even better: Jinshanling or Simatai (wild, unrestored, you'll be nearly alone). Arrive by 8 AM whatever you choose.

The Forbidden City sells out. Tickets are released 7 days in advance on the official WeChat mini-program. They sell out in hours during peak season. Book the exact moment they become available. If you miss it, try again at midnight — sometimes they release returned tickets.

Avoid public holidays. National Day (Oct 1-7), Chinese New Year (Jan/Feb), and May Day (May 1-5) transform Beijing into a wall-to-wall crowd. Everything is more expensive and twice as crowded. If you must come, book everything 2 months ahead.

Peking Duck for tourists vs locals. Quanjude is historic but touristy. Dadong is modern and refined. The local secret: small restaurants near Qianmen serve just-as-good duck for one-third the price.
honestTips.zh:
长城人很多。所有人都去八达岭——跟下饺子一样。去慕田峪（人少，有滑道下山）。更好的是金山岭或司马台（野长城，几乎没有游客）。不管去哪段，早上8点前到。

故宫门票靠抢。提前7天在官方微信小程序放票。旺季几小时就抢光。放票那一刻就去抢。没抢到的话凌晨再看看——有时候会有退票放出来。

避开公共假期。国庆（10月1-7日）、春节（1/2月）、五一（5月1-5日）期间北京人山人海。什么都贵，哪都挤。如果非来不可，提前两个月订好一切。

烤鸭去游客店还是本地店？全聚德有名但游客多。大董现代精致。本地秘密：前门附近的小店做的烤鸭不差，价格只有三分之一。

localSecrets.en:
Jingshan Park at sunset. Climb the hill behind the Forbidden City for the single best view of Beijing — the entire Forbidden City sprawled at your feet, golden rooftops catching the last light. Entrance is ¥2. Go 30 minutes before sunset.

Wudaoying Hutong. Skip Nanluoguxiang (too many tourists and mediocre overpriced snacks). Walk 10 minutes east to Wudaoying — quiet hutong with excellent independent cafes, bookstores, and restaurants that locals actually go to.

Temple of Heaven mornings. Locals gather at 6 AM for tai chi, dancing, kite flying, and an impromptu concert of traditional instruments. It's more entertaining than the temple itself. You don't need a ticket for the park; just the inner temple area costs ¥15.

Chinese breakfast on the street. Look for the breakfast carts between 6-9 AM. Try jianbing (savory crepe, ¥5-8), youtiao (fried dough), and doujiang (soy milk). Point at what you want, hand over small change — the best ¥5 you'll spend in Beijing.
localSecrets.zh:
景山公园看日落。故宫后门的小山包，是看北京最好的地方——整个故宫在脚下展开，金色屋顶被夕阳照亮。门票¥2。日落前半小时到。

五道营胡同。别去南锣鼓巷（太多游客，小吃贵还不好吃）。往东走10分钟到五道营——安静的胡同，有很好的独立咖啡馆、书店和本地人真的会去的餐馆。

天坛清晨。早上6点本地人就来打太极、跳舞、放风筝、还有即兴民乐演奏。比天坛本身还有看头。公园门票¥15，不进祈年殿不需要额外买票。

街头早点。早上6-9点找早摊车。尝尝煎饼果子（¥5-8）、油条、豆浆。指一下你要什么，给零钱——这是你在北京花得最值的¥5。
